description:
WHY: bl review is the proper squash-delivery path, but it cannot review any task that was claimed with --no-worktree in a git repo. In the bare balls repo every confirmation/archival task must be claimed --no-worktree, so the documented squash-delivery flow is unreachable here; agents are forced into the create -> 'bl update <id> status=closed --note' archival workaround instead of squash-delivered tasks. Distinct from bl-8cf7: that is Store::discover failing at the bare root ('not initialized'); THIS reproduces even when discovery works (run from a valid cwd such as .balls/worktree).

REPRO (from /home/mark/dev/balls/.balls/worktree so bl-8cf7 does not mask it):
  $ bl create x -t bug                       # -> bl-XXXX
  $ bl claim bl-XXXX --no-worktree --as repro
  claimed bl-XXXX (no worktree)
  $ bl review bl-XXXX -m repro
  error: git error: failed to spawn git: No such file or directory (os error 2)

ROOT CAUSE: src/commands/lifecycle.rs cmd_review branches only on store.no_git: no_git -> review_no_git, else -> review::review_worktree. There is no branch for a task claimed --no-worktree while a git repo is present. Such a task has task.branch = None and no worktree, but it still enters review_worktree, which resolves worktree_path(store,id) (src/worktree.rs) for a worktree that was never created and then invokes git with current_dir set to that nonexistent path. std::process::Command::spawn surfaces a missing current_dir as ENOENT, producing 'failed to spawn git: No such file or directory (os error 2)'. The bare repo just makes it 100% reproducible because there is no incidental fallback working dir.

FIX (decide intent first): either (a) give cmd_review a third path for --no-worktree-claimed git tasks that performs a metadata-only review transition mirroring review_no_git while still committing on the state branch (no squash, since there is no work branch to squash), or (b) if --no-worktree tasks are deliberately not squash-deliverable, fail fast with a clear message ('task bl-XXXX was claimed --no-worktree; nothing to squash - close via bl update <id> status=closed') instead of leaking an ENOENT. Reuse the bare-detection pattern already in src/bare_squash.rs. Relate to bl-8cf7 (same bare-repo blast radius; together they block the proper delivery path from this repo root).
title: bl review on a --no-worktree-claimed task spawns git in a missing dir → ENOENT
